Adhatoda, also known as Adolia discolor, is a tropical plant native to the Indian subcontinent, with a definition that includes its use as a medicinal herb. The leaves of the Adhatoda plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Adhatoda include alkaloids, particularly vasicine and vasicinone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Adhatoda is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
